Sizing a 5HP motor circuit at your facility? Here's the math nobody loves doing manually:

• Full Load Amps (FLA) — derated for ambient temp
• Locked Rotor Amps (LRA) — for inrush
• Conductor sizing per NEC 430.22
• Branch breaker per NEC 430.52

We built a free Power Calculator with PH 220V / 380V presets specifically for facility managers and maintenance leads. Plug in HP and voltage, get the recommended sizes in seconds.

No signup. No paywall. Built by Kinmo, the official UNI-T distributor in the Philippines.

The 220V/380V trick — a 30-second walkthrough.

Most online voltage calculators don't know what to do with PH 380V 3-phase. Ours does. Here's how to use it.

(Reel — record screen capture: open tool → type 5kW → switch system 1P 220V → 3P 380V → see currents change)

Solar installer? Stop estimating string current and pump amps with mental math.

The free Power Calculator handles:
• PV string sizing (current per voltage system)
• Off-grid pump motor + AC inverter loads
• Battery bank conversion math (kWh ↔ Ah at any voltage)
• Wire/breaker recommendation for hybrid systems

Built for the EPC + residential solar crowd. PH 220V/380V presets included.

Tag-init = brownouts. Brownouts = genset. Genset = LRA sizing.

Most facility folks size gensets by total kW load and pray the inrush doesn't trip. The right way: size for Locked Rotor Amps (LRA), which can be 6–8x running current for big motors.

Our free Power Calculator's Motor HP tab handles this:
✓ Enter motor HP
✓ Get FLA, SF amps, LRA
✓ Recommended branch breaker (NEC 430)
✓ Conductor size

In the brownout season especially, get this right BEFORE the genset specs go to procurement.

→ unit-philippines-blog.vercel.app/tools/power-calculator.html
